notes: Passengers on Cora's midnight bus. Most board at the front door at a scheduled mile-marker, ride for a while, and disembark at the next stop or the shoulder. Some are regulars.

visitors.name: The Late-Shift Worker
visitors.lore_text: Works the late shift at the warehouse on the bank. Boards every night at this stop. Gets off at the same stop. Cora has known their face for two years.

steps.greet: Nod when they board. They nod back.
steps.listen: They tell you about a shift-supervisor change.
steps.deliver: You hand them their transfer ticket.
steps.sit_with: You stop briefly at their stop. They thank you. They get off.

visitors.name: The First Passenger
visitors.lore_text: Boards at the first stop after the depot. Always pays exact change. Always sits up front so they can watch the road through the windshield.

steps.greet: They board. They drop the exact fare.
steps.listen: They ask you about the weather where you're going.
steps.deliver: You hand them a candy from the dish.
steps.sit_with: They stay near the front. You both look at the road for a while.

visitors.name: The Lost Traveler
visitors.lore_text: Someone who got on the wrong bus four stops ago and is trying to figure out where they should have gotten off. They have a paper schedule that's three years old.

steps.greet: They look at the route map taped to the dash.
steps.listen: They tell you where they were trying to go.
steps.deliver: You hand them the current paper schedule and circle the right stop.
steps.sit_with: You sit in the aisle with them for a minute. They thank you. They get off at the right stop.

visitors.name: The Kid Running Away
visitors.lore_text: Sixteen. Pays cash. Goes to the back row. Won't make eye contact. Has a backpack.

steps.greet: You watch them in the mirror. They don't look back.
steps.listen: You don't ask. They tell you anyway, halfway through the trip.
steps.deliver: You hand them a granola bar from the dashboard stash.
steps.sit_with: You sit at the back row with them for a stop. They get off at the next stop, not the one they paid for. They are going home.

visitors.name: The Old Regular
visitors.lore_text: An older man who's been on this bus for thirty years. Knows Cora's name. Knows the bus.

steps.listen: He tells you about a thing his wife said this morning.
steps.deliver: You hand him the warm spot near the heater vent in row 8.
steps.sit_with: You park briefly at his stop. He squeezes your shoulder. He gets off.

visitors.name: The Late Couple
visitors.lore_text: Two people who almost missed the last bus. They are quiet and grateful and tired.

steps.greet: They board. They both say thank you.
steps.listen: They tell you about the meeting they came from.
steps.sit_with: You hold the bus at their stop a beat longer. They both thank you again.

visitors.name: Cora (off-shift)
visitors.lore_text: When the player is NOT Cora, she gets on at the late-shift change and rides to the depot. When the player IS Cora, this entry swaps out.

steps.greet: She gets on at the depot driver-change. She knows you.
steps.listen: She tells you about a passenger from last week.
steps.deliver: You hand her a coffee from the dashboard thermos.
steps.sit_with: You sit in the aisle middle together. She is steady.
